subject: [PATCH v4 05/14] mm: add MM_CP_UFFD_RWP change_protection() flag

2026-05-25.patch_summary: This patch adds two new change_protection() flags to the Linux kernel: MM_CP_UFFD_RWP and MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE. The MM_CP_UFFD_RWP flag is used to install a PAGE_NONE PTE with the uffd bit set, which distinguishes it from other faults such as mprotect(PROT_NONE) or NUMA hinting faults. The MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE flag is used to restore the original vma->vm_page_prot and clear the uffd bit. These flags are wired through various functions including change_pte_range(), change_huge_pmd(), and hugetlb_change_protection() to ensure that anon, shmem, THP, and hugetlb all share the same semantics. This patch is a preparatory step for userfaultfd RWP functionality.
